IN THE MATTER OF an application by (Name) for the revocation or variation of an information demand under section 20.22 of the Canadian Security Intelligence Service Act, R.S.C. 1985, c. C-23
NOTICE

This is a notice that (name of person or entity named in the information demand) (âthe applicantâ) intends to apply to the Federal Court for the revocation or variation of the information demand made on (date).
Notice of the applicantâs intention has been provided to an employee of the Canadian Security Intelligence Service on (date).
The applicant intends to file the application for revocation or variation on or before (date).
Dated (date), at (place).
(Signature of applicant)

Section.Text: Form 5 of Schedule 2 to the Act is replaced by the following:

ScheduleFormHeading.Label: FORM 5
ScheduleFormHeading.TitleText: Notice â Application for Revocation or Variation of an Information Order or Production Order

Text.Emphasis: (Court File No. â to match information order or production order)

Provision.Text: FEDERAL COURT

IN THE MATTER OF an application by (Name) for the revocation or variation of an information order or production order under section 20.5 of the Canadian Security Intelligence Service Act, R.S.C. 1985, c. C-23
NOTICE

This is a notice that (name of person or entity named in the order) (âthe applicantâ) intends to apply to the Federal Court for the revocation or variation of the (specify: information order or production order) made on (date) and received by the applicant on (date).
A copy of this notice has been provided to an employee of the Canadian Security Intelligence Service on (date).
The applicant intends to file the application for revocation or variation on or before (date).
Dated (date), at (place).
(Signature of applicant)

Section.Text: Section 11 of the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.Text: Subsections 487(2.1) to (2.3) and (2.5) to (2.91) of the Criminal Code apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, with respect to a warrant issued under subsection (1).

Section.Text: Section 87 of the Cannabis Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.Text: Subsections 487(2.1) to (2.3) and (2.5) to (2.91) of the Criminal Code apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, with respect to a warrant issued under subsection (1).

Section.Text: This Part comes into force on the 90th day after the day on which this Act receives royal assent.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Supporting Authorized Access to Information Act

Section.Text: The Supporting Authorized Access to Information Act, whose text is as follows and whose schedule is set out in Schedule 2 to this Act, is enacted:

BillInternal.LongTitle: An Act respecting the obligations of electronic service providers in relation to authorized access to information

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Supporting Authorized Access to Information Act.

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: access,âin relation to information, means access by any means that may be authorized under the Criminal Code or the Canadian Security Intelligence Service Act, including by obtaining a document containing information and, with respect to information related to a communication, by intercepting the communication.â(accÃ¨s)
authorized personâmeans a person having authority under the Criminal Code or the Canadian Security Intelligence Service Act to access information.â(personne autorisÃ©e)
core providerâmeans an electronic service provider belonging to a class of electronic service providers set out in the schedule.â(fournisseur principal)
electronic protectionâmeans authentication, encryption and any other prescribed type of data protection.â(protection Ã©lectronique)

electronic serviceâmeans a service, or a feature of a service, that involves the creation, recording, storage, processing, transmission, reception, emission or making available of information in electronic, digital or any other intangible form by an electronic, digital, magnetic, optical, biometric, acoustic or other technological means, or a combination of any such means.â(service Ã©lectronique)
electronic service providerâmeans a person that, individually or as part of a group, provides an electronic service, including for the purpose of enabling communications, and that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: provides the service to persons in Canada; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: carries on all or part of its business activities in Canada.â(fournisseur de services Ã©lectroniques)

Definition.Text: informationâincludes any information, intelligence or data to which access may be authorized under the Criminal Code or the Canadian Security Intelligence Service Act.â(information)
interceptâhas the same meaning as in section 183 of the Criminal Code.â(intercepter)
Ministerâmeans the Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness.â(ministre)

personâincludes a corporation, a trust, a partnership, a fund, a joint venture, a government, a government agency, an unincorporated association or organization and any other legal entity.â(personne)
prescribedâmeans prescribed by the regulations.â(Version anglaise seulement)

Subsection.Text: Nothing in this Act derogates from any authority to access information conferred under the Criminal Code or the Canadian Security Intelligence Service Act â or from any similar authority conferred under another Act of Parliament â or from any corresponding obligation imposed on an electronic service provider to assist in a personâs exercise of such an authority.

Nothing in this Act derogates from any agreement or arrangement between an electronic service provider and a national security or law enforcement agency with respect to assistance to be provided to an authorized person in the exercise of the personâs authority to access information, nor prevents entry into such an agreement or arrangement.

Section.Text: The purpose of this Act is to ensure that electronic service providers can facilitate the exercise of authorities to access information that are conferred on authorized persons.
This Act is binding on His Majesty in right of Canada or of a province.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, by regulation, amend the schedule by adding, amending or deleting a class of electronic service providers.
The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the obligations of core providers, including regulations respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the development, implementation, assessment, testing and maintenance of operational and technical capabilities, including capabilities related to extracting and organizing information that is authorized to be accessed and to providing access to such information to authorized persons;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the installation, use, operation, management, assessment, testing and maintenance of any device, equipment or other thing that may enable an authorized person to access information; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: notices to be given to the Minister or other persons, including with respect to any capability referred to in paragraph (a) and any device, equipment or other thing referred to in paragraph (b).

Subsection.Text: A core provider is not required to comply with a provision of a regulation made under subsection (2), with respect to an electronic service, if compliance with that provision would require the provider to introduce a systemic vulnerability in electronic protections related to that service or prevent the provider from rectifying such a vulnerability.

On application by a core provider, the Minister may, by order and on any terms that the Minister considers necessary, exempt the core provider, for a specified period, from the application of any provision of a regulation made under subsection 5(2).
The application must be submitted in the form and manner specified by the Minister and must contain

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the provision with respect to which the exemption is sought;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a proposed period for the exemption;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a rationale explaining why the core provider requires the exemption;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a plan setting out the measures that the core provider intends to take to comply with the provision within the period referred to in paragraph (b); and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any other information specified by the Minister.

Subsection.Text: The provision with respect to which the exemption is sought does not apply to the core provider pending the determination of the application.
As soon as feasible after the application is submitted, the Minister must make a decision in respect of it and give written notice of the decision to the core provider.
In a decision to deny the application, the Minister may specify the day by which the core provider must comply with the provision with respect to which the application was denied.
The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to an order made under subsection (1).

Subject to sections 8 and 9, the Minister may make an order with respect to an electronic service provider and the order may contain any provision that may be contained in a regulation made under subsection 5(2), whether or not the provider is a core provider. The order must specify the period during which it has effect.
In making the order, the Minister must take into account the following factors:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the benefits of the order to the administration of justice, in particular to investigations under the Criminal Code, and to the performance of duties and functions under the Canadian Security Intelligence Service Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: whether complying with the order would be feasible for the electronic service provider;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the costs to be incurred by the electronic service provider to ensure compliance with the order;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the potential impact of the order on the persons to whom the electronic service provider provides services; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any other factor that the Minister considers relevant.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of offsetting all or part of the costs referred to in paragraph (2)(c), in the order, the Minister may provide for compensation to be paid to the electronic service provider in an amount that the Minister considers appropriate and may include provisions with respect to the time and manner of payment.

The electronic service provider is not required to comply with a provision of the order, with respect to an electronic service, if compliance with that provision would require the provider to introduce a systemic vulnerability in electronic protections related to that service or prevent the provider from rectifying such a vulnerability.
The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to an order made under subsection (1).

Section.Text: Before making an order under subsection 7(1), the Minister must provide the electronic service provider with an opportunity to make representations.
Before making an order under subsection 7(1), the Minister must consult the Minister of Industry.

Subsection.Text: Unless revoked earlier, an order made under subsection 7(1) has effect for the period specified in the order.
Sections 8 and 9 do not apply to the making of an order that only revokes another order made under subsection 7(1) or only extends the period specified in another order made under subsection 7(1).
Before the order expires, the Minister must review the order to determine whether, taking into account the factors referred to in subsection 7(2), the period specified in it should be extended.
Before the order expires, an electronic service provider subject to it may submit to the Minister any information that is relevant to the review.

Section.Text: An electronic service provider that is subject to an order made under subsection 7(1) must comply with it.
An order made under subsection 7(1) prevails over any regulation made under subsection 5(2) to the extent of any inconsistency.

Subsection.Text: On request made by a person referred to in subsection (2), an electronic service provider must provide all reasonable assistance, in any prescribed time and manner, to permit the assessment or testing of any device, equipment or other thing that may enable an authorized person to access information.
The request may be made by any of the following persons:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an employee of the Canadian Security Intelligence Service;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a person appointed or employed under Part I of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police Act or a civilian employee referred to in section 10 of that Act;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a civilian employee of another police force;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a peace officer, as defined in section 2 of the Criminal Code.

Section.Text: An electronic service provider and any person acting on its behalf must not disclose any of the following information except as permitted under this Act or the Canada Evidence Act:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: information contained in an order made under subsection 6(1) or 7(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: information on which the Minister relied in making the order;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the fact that the electronic service provider is subject to the order;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: information provided in the course of representations made under section 8 or in any response given by the Minister and the fact that the Minister has invited the representations;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: information contained in an application referred to in subsection 6(1) or in a decision made under subsection 6(4);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: information submitted under subsection 11(2) and any information received from the Minister in response;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: information related to a systemic vulnerability or potential systemic vulnerability in electronic protections employed by that electronic service provider;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: any prescribed information.

Section.MarginalNote: Judicial review â notice to Minister
Section.Text: An electronic service provider may not make an application for judicial review in respect of an order or decision made under this Act unless, at least 15 days before making the application, it provides written notice to the Minister, including a copy of the notice of application.

The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting confidentiality and security requirements with which electronic service providers and persons acting on their behalf must comply, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the disclosure of information referred to in section 15;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: establishing rules of procedure for the protection of information referred to in section 15 in administrative or judicial proceedings;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting requirements related to employees of electronic service providers and other persons whose services may be engaged by electronic service providers, including with respect to their security clearance and location; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting security requirements with respect to the facilities and premises of electronic service providers.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, subject to any restrictions or conditions that the Minister may specify, designate persons or classes of persons for the purposes of the administration and enforcement of this Act.
The Minister must provide every designated person with a certificate attesting to their designation.

Subject to subsection 20(1), a designated person may, for the purpose of verifying compliance or preventing non-compliance with this Act, enter any place if they have reasonable grounds to believe that anything relevant to that verification or prevention, including any document or electronic data, is located in that place or that an activity regulated by this Act is conducted in that place.
On entering a place referred to in subsection (1), the designated person must, on request, produce the certificate attesting to their designation to the person in charge of the place.
The designated person may, for a purpose referred to in subsection (1),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: examine anything found in the place, including any document or electronic data;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: make copies of any document or electronic data that is found in the place or take extracts from the document or electronic data;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: remove any document found in the place for examination or copying;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: use or cause to be used any computer or data processing system at the place to examine or copy electronic data; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: use or cause to be used any copying equipment at the place to make copies of any document.

Subsection.Text: If the designated person removes a document under paragraph (3)(c), they must return it to its owner or the person in charge of it on completion of the examination or copying.

Every owner or person in charge of a place that is entered by the designated person and every person found in the place must give all assistance that is reasonably required to enable the designated person to exercise their powers or perform their duties and functions under this section and provide the designated person with any document or electronic data that they may reasonably require.

The designated person may be accompanied by any other person who the designated person believes is necessary to help them exercise their powers or perform their duties and functions under this section.

The designated person and any person accompanying them may enter and pass through private property, other than a dwelling-house on that property, to gain entry to a place referred to in subsection (1). For greater certainty, they are not liable for doing so.
In the case of a dwelling-house, a designated person is not authorized to enter it without the occupantâs consent except under the authority of a warrant issued under subsection (2).

A justice of the peace may, on ex parte application, issue a warrant authorizing the designated person named in it to enter a dwelling-house, subject to any conditions specified in the warrant, if the justice of the peace is satisfied by information on oath that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the dwelling-house is a place referred to in subsection 19(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: entry to the dwelling-house is necessary for a purpose referred to in subsection 19(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: entry was refused by the occupant or there are reasonable grounds to believe that entry will be refused by, or that consent to entry cannot be obtained from, the occupant.

Subsection.Text: In executing the warrant, the designated person is not entitled to use force unless the use of force is specifically authorized in the warrant and they are accompanied by a peace officer.

Subject to the regulations, a designated person may, for a purpose related to verifying compliance or preventing non-compliance with this Act, make an order in writing requiring an electronic service provider to conduct, within the time and in the manner specified in the order, an internal audit of its practices, documents and electronic data to determine whether it is in compliance with any provision of this Act or the regulations.
The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to an order made under subsection (1).

Section.Text: The electronic service provider must comply with the order and provide to the designated person, within the period specified in the order, a report of the results of the audit, including, if the electronic service provider determines that there is non-compliance with a provision of this Act or the regulations, the nature of the non-compliance and any measures that it has taken or will take to comply with the provision or the order.

Section.MarginalNote: Power to order termination of contravention

Subsection.Text: If a designated person believes on reasonable grounds that there is or is likely to be a contravention of any provision of this Act or the regulations, they may make an order in writing requiring an electronic service provider to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: stop doing something that is or is likely to be in contravention of that provision or cause it to be stopped; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: take any measure that is necessary to comply with the requirements of that provision or mitigate the effects of non-compliance.

Subsection.Text: The order must specify the time within which and the manner in which the electronic service provider may request a review of the order by the Minister.
The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to an order made under subsection (1).
An electronic service provider that is subject to an order made under subsection 23(1) must comply with it.
Once the electronic service provider complies with the order, it must notify the designated person of its compliance without delay.
An order that is made under subsection 23(1) must be reviewed by the Minister at the written request of the electronic service provider that is subject to the order.
The request must be made within the time and in the manner specified in the order and must state the grounds for review and set out the evidence that supports those grounds.
The order continues to apply during a review unless the Minister decides otherwise.
On completion of a review, the Minister must confirm, amend, revoke or cancel the order and provide notice of the decision and the reasons for the decision to the electronic service provider.

If the Minister does not make a decision in respect of the request within 90 days after the day on which the request is received, or within any further period that is agreed on by the Minister and the electronic service provider, the Minister is deemed to have confirmed the order.

Section.Text: The purpose of an administrative monetary penalty is to promote compliance with this Act and not to punish.

Subsection.Text: A person who contravenes any of the following provisions commits a violation and is liable to a penalty of an amount that is to be established by a designated person in accordance with the regulations:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: section 12, subsection 14(1), section 15, subsection 19(5), section 22 or subsection 24(1) or (2);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a provision of the regulations designated by a regulation made under paragraph 46(1)(e).

Subsection.Text: The maximum penalty for a violation is $50,000 in the case of an individual and $250,000 in the case of any other person.
A violation that is committed or continued on more than one day constitutes a separate violation in respect of each day on which it is committed or continued.
A person is not to be found liable for a violation if they establish that they exercised due diligence to prevent its commission.

Every rule and principle of the common law that renders any circumstance a justification or excuse in relation to a charge for an offence under this Act applies in respect of a violation to the extent that it is not inconsistent with this Act.

Section.Text: In any proceedings under this Act against a person in relation to a violation, it is sufficient proof of the violation to establish that the act or omission that constitutes the violation was committed by an employee or an agent or mandatary of the person, whether or not the employee or agent or mandatary is identified.

If a person other than an individual commits a violation under this Act, any of the personâs directors, officers or agents or mandataries who directed, authorized, assented to, acquiesced in or participated in the commission of the violation is a party to and liable for the violation, whether or not the person who actually committed the violation is proceeded against under this Act.
If a designated person believes on reasonable grounds that a person has committed a violation, the designated person may issue, and must serve the person with, a notice of violation.

Subsection.Text: A notice of violation may not be issued in respect of a violation more than two years after the day on which the Minister becomes aware of the acts or omissions that constitute the alleged violation.

A document appearing to have been issued by the Minister, certifying the day on which the acts or omissions that constitute the alleged violation became known to the Minister, is admissible in evidence without proof of the signature or official character of the person appearing to have signed the document and, in the absence of evidence to the contrary, is proof that the Minister became aware of the acts or omissions on that day.

Section.MarginalNote: How act or omission may be proceeded with
Section.Text: If an act or omission may be proceeded with either as a violation or as an offence, proceeding in one manner precludes proceeding in the other.

Heading.TitleText: Payment of Penalties and Alternatives to Payment

Subsection.Text: If a person named in a notice of violation pays, in the prescribed time and manner, the penalty set out in the notice of violation,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: they are deemed to have committed the violation in respect of which the amount is paid;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister must accept that amount as complete satisfaction of the penalty; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended.

Subsection.Text: Instead of paying the penalty, the person may, in the prescribed time and manner,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: request to enter into a compliance agreement with the Minister that ensures the personâs compliance with the provision to which the violation relates; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: request a review by the Minister of the acts or omissions that constitute the alleged violation or the amount of the penalty.

Subsection.Text: If the person does not pay the penalty in the prescribed time and manner and does not make one of the requests referred to in subsection (2) in the prescribed time and manner, they are deemed to have committed the violation identified in the notice.

The Minister may, on request, enter into a compliance agreement with a person named in a notice of violation on any conditions that are satisfactory to the Minister. The compliance agreement may provide for the reduction, in whole or in part, of the amount of the penalty for the violation.
A person who enters into a compliance agreement with the Minister is, on doing so, deemed to have committed the violation in respect of which the compliance agreement was entered into.

If the Minister is satisfied that a person who has entered into a compliance agreement has complied with it, the Minister must cause a notice to that effect to be provided to the person, at which time the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended.

If the Minister is of the opinion that a person who has entered into a compliance agreement has not complied with it, the Minister must cause a notice of default to be provided to the person to the effect that, instead of the penalty set out in the notice of violation in respect of which the compliance agreement was entered into, the person is liable to pay, in the prescribed time and manner, twice the amount of that penalty, and, for greater certainty, subsection 28(2) does not apply in respect of that amount.

Once provided with the notice of default, the person may not deduct from the amount set out in the notice any amount that they spent under the compliance agreement and the person is liable to pay the amount set out in the notice.

If the person pays the amount set out in the notice of default, the Minister must accept the amount as complete satisfaction of the amount owing and the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended.

Section.MarginalNote: Refusal to enter into compliance agreement

Subsection.Text: If the Minister refuses to enter into a compliance agreement with a person named in a notice of violation, the person is liable to pay, in the prescribed time and manner, the penalty set out in the notice of violation.
If the person pays the penalty,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: they are deemed to have committed the violation in respect of which the payment is made;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister must accept the amount as complete satisfaction of the penalty; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended.

Subsection.Text: If the person does not pay the penalty in the prescribed time and manner, they are deemed to have committed the violation identified in the notice of violation.

On completion of a review requested under paragraph 34(2)(b) with respect to the acts or omissions that constitute the alleged violation, the Minister must determine, on a balance of probabilities, whether the person named in the notice of violation committed the violation. If the Minister determines that the person committed the violation but that the amount of the penalty was not established by the designated person in accordance with the regulations, the Minister must correct the amount.
If the Minister determines that the person named in the notice of violation did not commit the violation, the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Review â with respect to penalty
Subsection.Text: On completion of a review requested under paragraph 34(2)(b) with respect to the amount of the penalty, the Minister must determine whether the amount of the penalty was established in accordance with the regulations and, if not, the Minister must correct the amount.
The Minister must cause a notice of any decision made under subsection (1) or (3) to be provided to the person named in the notice of violation.

The person named in the notice of violation is liable to pay, in the time and manner specified in a decision made under subsection (1) or (3), the amount of the penalty that is confirmed or corrected in the decision.

If the person named in the notice of violation pays the amount referred to in subsection (5), the Minister must accept the amount as complete satisfaction of the penalty and the proceedings commenced in respect of the violation are ended.
The following amounts constitute debts due to His Majesty in right of Canada that may be recovered in the Federal Court:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of a penalty, beginning on the day on which the notice of violation setting out the amount of the penalty is served;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount set out in a compliance agreement entered into under subsection 35(1), beginning on the day on which the compliance agreement is entered into;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the amount set out in a notice of default referred to in subsection 35(4), beginning on the day on which the time the notice is provided; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of a penalty as set out in a decision made by the Minister under subsection 37(1) or (3), beginning on the day on which the time the notice of that decision is provided.

Subsection.Text: No proceedings to recover such a debt may be commenced later than five years after the debt became payable.
The debt is final and not subject to review or to be restrained, prohibited, removed, set aside or otherwise dealt with except to the extent and in the manner provided by sections 34 to 37.
Any debt referred to in subsection 38(1) in respect of which there is a default of payment, or the part of any such debt that has not been paid, may be certified by the Minister.

Registration in the Federal Court of the certificate has the same force and effect as a judgment of that court for a debt of the amount specified in the certificate and all related registration costs.
A person who contravenes any of the following provisions commits an offence:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: section 12, subsection 14(1), section 15, subsection 19(5), section 22 or subsection 24(1) or (2);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a provision of the regulations designated by a regulation made under paragraph 46(1)(f).

Subsection.Text: A person who commits an offence under subsection (1) is liable on summary conviction to a fine of not more than $100,000 in the case of an individual and not more than $500,000 in the case of any other person.
If an offence under subsection (1) is committed or continued on more than one day, it constitutes a separate offence for each day on which it is committed or continued.
A person is not to be found guilty of an offence under subsection (1) if they establish that they exercised due diligence to prevent its commission.

Section.Text: In a prosecution of a person for an offence under subsection 40(1), it is sufficient proof of the offence to establish that the act or omission that constitutes the offence was committed by an employee or an agent or mandatary of the person, whether or not the employee or agent or mandatary is identified.

If a person other than an individual commits an offence under subsection 40(1), any of the personâs directors, officers or agents or mandataries who directed, authorized, assented to, acquiesced in or participated in the commission of the offence is a party to the offence and is liable on conviction to the punishment provided for by this Act, whether or not the person who actually committed the offence is prosecuted.
A person commits an offence if they knowingly obstruct or hinder any of the following persons who are carrying out functions or duties under this Act:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person referred to in subsection 14(2);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person designated under subsection 18(1).

Section.Text: A person commits an offence if they knowingly make a false or misleading statement, verbally or in writing, to any of the following persons who are carrying out functions or duties under this Act:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person referred to in subsection 14(2);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person designated under subsection 18(1).

Section.Text: A person who commits an offence under section 43 or 44 is liable on summary conviction

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine of not more than $25,000 for a first offence and of not more than $50,000 for each subsequent offence; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of any other person, to fine of not more than $100,000 for a first offence and of not more than $250,000 for each subsequent offence.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting any fees payable to electronic service providers for different types of assistance that they provide to persons exercising an authority referred to in subsection 2(2);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting record-keeping and reporting by electronic service providers;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the meaning of any term or expression for the purposes of this Act, including âauthenticationâ, âencryptionâ and âsystemic vulnerabilityâ;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting orders referred to in section 21 and reports referred to in section 22;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the administrative monetary penalties scheme, including regulations

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: designating provisions of the regulations for the purposes of paragraph 28(1)(b),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: respecting the establishment of penalty amounts,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: respecting the content of notices of violation,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: respecting reviews by the Minister, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: respecting the service or provision of documents;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: designating provisions of the regulations for the purposes of paragraph 40(1)(b);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing anything that is required or authorized by this Act to be prescribed;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the way in which anything that is required or authorized by this Act to be prescribed is to be determined; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: generally, for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Act.

Subsection.Text: Every five years after the day on which the first regulation is made under paragraph (1)(a), the Minister must review all regulations made under that paragraph.

Before the first regulation under paragraph (1)(a) is made and during every review referred to in subsection (2), the Minister must publish a notice inviting the following persons to make representations within the time and in the form and manner specified in the notice:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Attorney General of Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the attorney general of each province;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: core providers; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: electronic service providers that are subject to orders made under subsection 7(1).

Section.Text: For greater certainty, regulations made under this Act may establish classes of electronic service providers and distinguish among them, including on the basis of the electronic service provided and on the basis of the number of persons in Canada to whom the electronic service provider provides services.
This Part comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Heading.Label: PART 16
Heading.TitleText: Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act (Collection and Use of Personal Information)

Section.Text: The Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act is amended by adding the following after section 11.7:

Heading.Label: PART 1.2
Heading.TitleText: Collection and Use of Personal Information

Subsection.Text: A person or entity referred to in section 5 may collect an individualâs personal information without the individualâs knowledge or consent if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the personal information is disclosed to the person or entity by the Royal Canadian Mounted Police or by a prescribed government department, institution or agency or a prescribed law enforcement agency; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the discloser of the personal information affirms to the person or entity in writing that it considers that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the disclosure is for the purpose of detecting or deterring money laundering, terrorist activity financing or sanctions evasion,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the disclosure is made without the individualâs knowledge or consent, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: making the disclosure with the individualâs knowledge or consent would compromise the ability to detect or deter money laundering, terrorist activity financing or sanctions evasion.

Subsection.Text: The discloser may, in its written affirmation, set out an additional purpose that is consistent with the purposes set out in subparagraph (1)(b)(i).

A person or entity referred to in section 5 may, without an individualâs knowledge or consent, use the individualâs personal information collected under section 11.71 only for the purposes for which it was disclosed to the person or entity or for the purpose of detecting or deterring a contravention of the laws of Canada or a province that relates to money laundering, terrorist activity financing or sanctions evasion.
For greater certainty, the person or entity must not use the personal information with the intent to prejudice a criminal investigation, whether or not a criminal investigation has begun.

Section.Text: No criminal or civil proceedings lie against a person or an entity that, in good faith, collects or uses personal information under this Part.
Nothing in this Part limits or affects any other lawful authority to disclose, collect or use an individualâs personal information without the individualâs knowledge or consent.

Heading.TitleText: Related Amendments to the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act

Subsection.Text: Subsection 7(1) of the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b.01):

Paragraph.Label: (b.02)
Paragraph.Text: the collection is made in accordance with section 11.71 of the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 7(2) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (c.1), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: it is used in accordance with section 11.72 of the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act.

Section.Text: Paragraph 9(3)(c.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: the information was collected under paragraphÂ 7(1)(b) or (b.02);

TableGroup.Caption: TABLE 3 â Preparations and Mixtures Containing Precursors

row.entry: Any preparation or mixture that contains phenethyl bromide ((2-bromoethyl)benzene)
Any preparation or mixture that contains propionic anhydride (propanoic anhydride)
Any preparation or mixture that contains benzyl chloride ((chloromethyl)benzene)
